q.en: What is the scientific name for hoofed animals?
q.zh: 有蹄动物的科学名称是什么？

explain.en: All hoofed animals belong to a group of mammals called ungulates.
explain.zh: 所有有蹄动物都属于一类叫“有蹄类（ungulates）”的哺乳动物。

q.en: What material are hooves made of?
q.zh: 蹄子是由什么物质构成的？

options.en: Bone only according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 纯骨头（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Hooves are made of keratin, the same material as hair, claws, and fingernails.
explain.zh: 蹄子由角蛋白构成，和毛发、爪子、指甲是同一种物质。

q.en: What is the 'cannon bone' in hoofed animals?
q.zh: 有蹄动物的“管骨”（cannon bone）是什么？

options.en: One long, solid foot bone connecting the hoof to the ankle
options.zh: 连接蹄与踝部的一根长而坚实的骨头

options.en: A horn on the head according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 头上的角（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The cannon bone is a single long, solid bone that makes legs longer and stronger for running.
explain.zh: 管骨是一根长而坚实的骨头，让腿更长更稳，便于奔跑。

q.en: Which group includes horses, zebras, and rhinos?
q.zh: 哪一类动物包含马、斑马和犀牛？

explain.en: Horses/zebras (one toe) and rhinos (three toes) are odd-toed ungulates.
explain.zh: 马、斑马（一趾）和犀牛（三趾）属于奇蹄目。

q.en: On which continents do even-toed ungulates NOT naturally live?
q.zh: 偶蹄目有蹄动物在自然状态下不生活在哪块大陆？

options.en: Europe and North America according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 欧洲和北美（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Even-toed ungulates are native to every continent except Australia and Antarctica.
explain.zh: 偶蹄目有蹄动物原生于除澳大利亚和南极洲以外的所有大陆。

q.en: Why do zebras have bold black-and-white stripes?
q.zh: 斑马为什么有醒目的黑白条纹？

options.en: To show they are poisonous according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 表示它们有毒（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: For camouflage by blending with the herd
options.zh: 通过融入群体起到伪装作用

explain.en: A zebra's stripes help it blend in with other zebras, making it harder for predators to single it out.
explain.zh: 斑马的条纹帮助它融入群体，使捕食者更难锁定单只个体。

q.en: What are rhino horns made of?
q.zh: 犀牛角是由什么构成的？

explain.en: Rhino horns are made of keratin, the same material as hooves.
explain.zh: 犀牛角由角蛋白构成，和蹄子成分相同。

q.en: Which kind of deer has antlers in both males and females?

explain.en: Caribou are the only deer in which the females also grow antlers.
explain.zh: 驯鹿是唯一雌雄都长角的鹿。

q.en: About how much plant food can an adult hippo eat in one day?
q.zh: 一头成年河马一天能吃多少植物？

options.en: None; they eat meat according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 不吃；它们吃肉（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: An adult hippo can eat up to 136 kg (300 lb) of plants in a single day.
explain.zh: 成年河马一天最多可吃下136公斤（300磅）植物。

q.en: What do most hoofed animals eat, since they are herbivores?
q.zh: 既然大多数是草食动物，有蹄动物主要吃什么？

explain.en: The book emphasizes that most hoofed animals are herbivores, which shapes their body design for grazing and fleeing.
explain.zh: 本书强调大多数有蹄动物是草食动物，这也决定了它们为吃草和逃跑而演化的身体结构。

prompt.en: The book divides hoofed animals into odd-toed and even-toed groups based on their toes. Choose one animal from the book and explain two body features that help it survive in its habitat.
prompt.zh: 本书按脚趾数量把有蹄动物分为奇蹄和偶蹄两类。请从书中选一种动物，说明有助于它在栖息地生存的两个身体特征。

en: Pick one animal (e.g., camel, giraffe, horse) and name its group (odd- or even-toed).
Describe two features (hooves, horns, long neck, humps, etc.) and how each helps it survive.
zh: 选一种动物（如骆驼、长颈鹿、马），并说明它属于奇蹄还是偶蹄。
描述两个特征（蹄、角、长颈、驼峰等），以及各自如何帮助它生存。
